session_1.user_goal: I needed to install the water filter and to teach her to do the same.
session_1.scenario:
I got into a silly fight with my wife this morning regarding the water filter. She didn't know how to screw it into the water jug. I didn't understand her at first. Maybe because I was still waking up and was groggy. I grew impatient and started to criticize her. I lost my temper, something I regret deeply.
Part of the problem is that my wife has attention deficit hyperactivity disorder and English is not her first language. So communication is a problem. But that is no excuse for my behavior. In the end, we made up, but I still feel ashamed.
Actionabl_Areas_Initial: I grew impatient and started to criticize her.
I lost my temper, something I regret deeply.
User_Plans_Initial: I should have taken a deep breath and listened to her more patiently.
I should have tried to see the humor in the situation.
User_Plans_Final: I should have taken a pause to calm myself down.
I could have taken a deep breath.

LLM-CT.item: I could have paused and physically shown her how to line up and screw the filter into the jug step by step, then had her try it herself while I guided her calmly.

LLM-CF.item: I could have stopped criticizing her and shown her step by step how to screw the filter into the jug, then had her try it herself while I guided her calmly.

LLM-B.item: I could have stayed calm and patiently shown her step by step how to screw the water filter into the jug, then let her try it herself while I explained it more clearly.

session_2.user_goal: To re-establish control of the classroom and move on.
session_2.scenario:
Today at the university a student in class was second-guessing nearly everything I mentioned in class and challenged me. I was patient, but then I lost my cool somewhat. In retrospect, I should have listened and told him we could discuss the matter outside of class.
But the problem is that the student talks a lot. He's intelligent, but he's highly opinionated and is quite full of himself. It's very hard to deal with people like that. I need to be respectful, but at the same time I want to be able to control the classroom.
Actionabl_Areas_Initial: I was patient, but then I lost my cool somewhat.
In retrospect, I should have listened and told him we could discuss the matter outside of class.
User_Plans_Initial: I should have told him that we need to move on to the main issue under discussion.
User_Plans_Final: I could have calmly acknowledged his point, told him I wanted to keep the class on track, and invited him to continue the discussion with me after class.

LLM-CT.item: At the start of class, I could have set a clear participation rule that brief questions were welcome but extended challenges would be parked for office hours or after class, so I had a neutral structure to refer back to when he began dominating the discussion.

LLM-CF.item: I could have kept my tone calm and neutral when I felt myself getting frustrated, instead of showing that I was losing my cool.

LLM-B.item: I could have calmly acknowledged his point, told him I wanted to keep the class on track, and invited him to continue the discussion with me after class.

session_3.user_goal: Improve the relationship with my son.
session_3.scenario:
I had a conversation with my son the other day about his request to see a psychologist. The conversation went relatively well until I told him that I wanted to enjoy spending time with him. He replied that I was making the conversation all about myself and my needs.
I realized then that I should have been more open to my son's concerns. As much as I love him, I need to be more considerate and listen more to him. I also need to stop talking so much and let my son talk instead. I'm confident once I do that, our relationship will improve.
Actionabl_Areas_Initial: The conversation went relatively well until I told him that I wanted to enjoy spending time with him.
I realized then that I should have been more open to my son's concerns.
User_Plans_Initial: I should have stopped talking and let him speak.
I should have put the focus on him entirely instead of mentioning my needs.
User_Plans_Final: I could have focused on listening to my son’s feelings about seeing a psychologist without bringing up my own desire to enjoy time together.

LLM-CT.item: I could have started the conversation by asking him what he was hoping to get from seeing a psychologist and what kind of support he wanted from me before I shared any of my own thoughts.

LLM-CF.item: I could have responded to his request to see a psychologist by asking a calm follow-up question like, \"Can you help me understand what’s been feeling hard for you lately and what kind of support you’re hoping for?\"

LLM-B.item: I could have focused on listening to my son’s feelings about seeing a psychologist without bringing up my own desire to enjoy time together.

feedback.1: I found the system helpful. But some of the recommendations were pretty self-evident.
feedback.2: I found that the AI suggestions confirmed my thinking rather than influencing my final decision.
feedback.3: I don't one should be required to type down one's feelings but speak into a microphone. Still, I did find the AI model of some use.
